findings.evidence: Mathlib defines `AddCircle` as `𝕜 ⧸ zmultiples p` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Topology/Instances/AddCircle/Defs.lean:192-194`; the PR proof uses only `QuotientAddGroup.eq_iff_sub_mem` at `AddCircle.lean:60-62` and subgroup translations at `AddCircle.lean:66-125`.
findings.fix: First prove the general quotient statement, e.g. for `H : AddSubgroup 𝕜`, `[PreconnectedSpace 𝕜] [TotallyDisconnectedSpace H]`, `Multiplicative H ≃* Deck (QuotientAddGroup.mk' H : 𝕜 → 𝕜 ⧸ H)`, with corresponding `mem`/translation lemmas, then define the `AddCircle` result by specializing `H := zmultiples p`.
findings.issue: `addCircleMulEquiv` is proved only for `AddCircle p`, even though the argument uses only that `AddCircle p` is `𝕜 ⧸ zmultiples p` and `QuotientAddGroup.eq_iff_sub_mem`; this leaves the API at a special-case level.

summary: The deck computation is specialized to `AddCircle p`/`zmultiples p`, but the proof is really the natural quotient-by-an-additive-subgroup statement. Generalize the quotient deck API first and derive the additive-circle corollary from it.
